title: Information of a Seafarer's Family or Next of Kin. In critical incidents,
content: 35 such as, but not limited to, accidents or deaths on board or offshore, piracy, abandonment 36 of vessel, missing seafarers and other similar cases, the shipowner/principal and the LMA 37 shall immediately inform the seafarer's family or next of kin, including any investigation 38 conducted, and shall furnish them with copies of any reports, action/s taken, and plans 39 by the shipowner and the manning agency concerned with regard to remedies, mitigation, 40 or repatriation. 41 42 For overseas seafarers, the shipowner and the concerned manning agency shall report 43 the critical incident to the DMW within five (5) calendar days from being informed of the 44 critical incident. The shipowner and the manning agency shall immediately provide the 45 appropriate assistance to the overseas seafarers as required by applicable laws, rules 46 and regulations. 47 48 In addition, for overseas seafarers, the shipowner and the manning agency shall provide 49 periodic bi-monthly reports of the action taken to the seafarer’s family or the next of kin 50 and the DMW as applicable until the matter is resolved. Failure to comply with the 51 foregoing reportorial requirements shall be ground for the imposition of the appropriate 52 penalties, as prescribed by the DMW. 53 54

metadata.source_file: [NOTICE TO PUBLIC] CLEAN DRAFT OF THE DMW SEABASED RULES.pdf
